description: In a VSF stack, the switch may fail to log events for the matching access-list entries. ACL functionality is not impacted; access-list entries are applied properly and only the logging is incorrectly generated.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what is the caveat related to ACLs in a VSF stack?

Feature Caveat: ACLs - In a VSF stack, the switch may fail to log events for matching access-list entries. ACL functionality is not impacted; access-list entries are applied properly and only the logging is incorrectly generated.

description: VSF stack formation is blocked when there are reserved autojoin interfaces (25, 26, 49, 50) in the stack topology.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what caveat is documented for Aruba CX Mobile App?
Feature Caveat: Aruba CX Mobile App - VSF stack formation is blocked when there are reserved autojoin interfaces (25, 26, 49, 50) in the stack topology.

description: When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will be disconnected. This cycle of connecting and disconnecting continues until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To prevent this unnecessary reconnection, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what caveat is documented for Certificates?

Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

description: For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what caveat is documented for Classifiers?

Feature Caveat: Classifiers - For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

description: Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what limitation is mentioned for Classifiers?
Feature Caveat: Classifiers - Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

description: No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what caveat is documented for CMF?
CMF - No other checkpoint besides 'startup-configuration' gets migrated during the upgrade process.

feature: DHCP Server, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Snooping
description: DHCP Relay and DHCP Snooping can co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping and DHCP Server cannot co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Server together cannot co-exist on the same switch.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what caveat is documented for DHCP Server, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Snooping?

Feature Caveat: DHCP Server, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Snooping - DHCP Relay and DHCP Snooping can co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping and DHCP Server cannot co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Server together cannot co-exist on the same switch.

description: When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

description: The switch may only software forward at a rate of 100pps if the packets that trigger ICMP redirect.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, for HPE Aruba AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what caveat is documented for ICMP Redirect?
Feature Caveat: ICMP Redirect - The switch may only software forward at a rate of 100pps if the packets that trigger ICMP redirect.

description: Reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services within the system are not recommended to be used for other services. When two services use the same port there is chance of unexpected behaviors from these services. Best practice is to use unique port for each service across system.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what caveat is documented for IP-SLA?

Feature Caveat: IP-SLA - Reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services within the system are not recommended to be used for other services. When two services use the same port there is a chance of unexpected behaviors from these services. Best practice is to use unique ports for each service across the system.

description: (for 8360 Switch Series only) In an environment with devices running AOS-Switch, do not enable UDLD on the same link. The UDLD session can toggle between up and down continuously when both MACsec and UDLD is enabled on the same link.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what caveat is documented for MACsec and UDLD?

Feature Caveat: MACsec and UDLD - (for 8360 Switch Series only) In an environment with devices running AOS-Switch, do not enable UDLD on the same link. The UDLD session can toggle between up and down continuously when both MACsec and UDLD is enabled on the same link.

description: When Cisco AnyConnect is used as dot1x supplicant, it is recommended to configure cak-length to be 16 under dot1x-authenticator mode.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what caveat is documented for MACsec?
Feature Caveat: MACsec - When Cisco AnyConnect is used as dot1x supplicant, it is recommended to configure cak-length to be 16 under dot1x-authenticator mode.

description: Ensure the cipher suite GCM-AES-128 is configured when AOS-CX is acting as a key server. This is because, by default AOS-CX will use the most secure cipher suite gcm-aes-xpn-256 for establishing MACsec secure link and Comware/PVOS doesn’t support an XPN cipher suite.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what limitation is mentioned for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - Ensure the cipher suite GCM-AES-128 is configured when AOS-CX is acting as a key server. This is because, by default AOS-CX will use the most secure cipher suite gcm-aes-xpn-256 for establishing MACsec secure link and Comware/PVOS doesn’t support an XPN cipher suite.

description: MACsec works between a CX device and a Windows VM running AnyConnect with AES-128 cipher. AnyConnect does not support AES-256 in the NAM module (works only for the VPN module).

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, for HPE Aruba AOS-CX release 10.14.0006, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - MACsec works between a CX device and a Windows VM running AnyConnect with AES-128 cipher. AnyConnect does not support AES-256 in the NAM module (works only for the VPN module).

description: In an environment with Cisco devices, when the GCM-AES-XPN-128 or GCM-AES-XPN-256 cipher suite is used for establishing the MACsec channel, the MKA policy on the Cisco device must be configured with ssci-based-on-sci.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - In an environment with Cisco devices, when the GCM-AES-XPN-128 or GCM-AES-XPN-256 cipher suite is used for establishing the MACsec channel, the MKA policy on the Cisco device must be configured with ssci-based-on-sci.

description: MACsec uses a software-based implementation to track start and stop times for secure channels and secure associations. As the implementation is software-based, the stop times for MACsec secure channel and secure associations are only updated when they are deleted and therefore never updated in the output of the show macsec status detailed command.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - The implementation uses a software-based approach to track start and stop times for secure channels and associations. Consequently, the stop times are only updated when the secure channel or association is deleted, and thus are not reflected in the output of the show macsec status detailed command.

description: In an environment with a Cisco device, the Cisco device must be designated as the key server. Designating the AOS-CX as the key server results in complete traffic loss.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?
Feature Caveat: MACsec - In an environment with a Cisco device, the Cisco device must be designated as the key server. Designating the AOS-CX as the key server results in complete traffic loss.

description: In an environment with Cisco and FlexFabric or H3C devices, do not update confidentiality-offset on the live channel. There can be complete traffic loss for an extended period on the MACsec channel when confidentiality-offset is updated on both ends.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what additional caveat is documented for MACsec?

Feature Caveat: MACsec - In an environment with Cisco and FlexFabric or H3C devices, do not update confidentiality-offset on the live channel. There can be complete traffic loss for an extended period on the MACsec channel when confidentiality-offset is updated on both ends.

description: OSPFv2 and OSPFv3 do not support detailed LSA show commands.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what OSPF caveat is documented?
Feature Caveat: OSPF - OSPFv2 and OSPFv3 do not support detailed LSA show commands.

description: Authorization by means of HPE VSAs is not supported.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what RADIUS caveat is documented?
Feature Caveat: RADIUS - Authorization by means of HPE VSAs is not supported.

description: REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what is the caveat related to REST?
REST supports 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what is the caveat related to SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

description: The switch does not support double encapsulation. A packet can be encapsulated with either L2GRE (UBT) or VXLAN, but not both. The network admin should decide the tunneling type to be used, and then plan the configuration accordingly.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what caveat is documented for User Based Tunnel?

Feature Caveat: User Based Tunnel - The switch does not support double encapsulation. A packet can be encapsulated with either L2GRE (UBT) or VXLAN, but not both. The network admin should decide the tunneling type to be used, and then plan the configuration accordingly.

description: In the event of license issues when onboarding a DUT to primary or backup mobility conductor, the DUT will not try to bootstrap to another mobility conductor where a license is available. For example, if a mobility conductor does not have a license to onboard the DUT but the backup mobility conductor does have adequate licenses, if both mobility conductors are reachable then UBT will be down, and the DUT will not attempt to bootstrap to the backup controller. However, if the primary mobility conductor is not reachable, the DUT gets tunneled to the standby/backup mobility conductor. Once the primary mobility conductor is reachable by the DUT once again, the DUT will not automatically bootstrap back to the primary. Network administrators should manually disable and enable UBT on the DUT to re-establish the tunnel to the primary mobility conductor.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what limitation is mentioned for User Based Tunnel in AOS-CX 10.14.0006?

Feature Caveat: User Based Tunnel - In the event of license issues when onboarding a DUT to primary or backup mobility conductor, the DUT will not try to bootstrap to another mobility conductor where a license is available. For example, if a mobility conductor does not have a license to onboard the DUT but the backup mobility conductor does have adequate licenses, if both mobility conductors are reachable then UBT will be down, and the DUT will not attempt to bootstrap to the backup controller. However, if the primary mobility conductor is not reachable, the DUT gets tunneled to the standby/backup mobility conductor. Once the primary mobility conductor is reachable by the DUT once again, the DUT will not automatically bootstrap back to the primary. Network administrators should manually disable and enable UBT on the DUT to re-establish the tunnel to the primary mobility conductor.

description: The R8V13A 6200F Switch does not support mixed VSF stacking.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what caveat is documented for VSF?
Feature Caveat: VSF - The R8V13A 6200F Switch does not support mixed VSF stacking.

description: Only static VXLAN is supported.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what caveat is documented for VXLAN?
Feature Caveat: VXLAN - Only static VXLAN is supported.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.
symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX.
scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features.
workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.
